entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop studied for its water relations and growth under specific soil and weather conditions

entities.name: DEEP VERTISOLS
entities.description: The type of soil on which the field studies were conducted

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: The limitation of water availability affecting plant growth and development

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The overall development and increase in size of the sorghum plant

entities.name: DRY MATTER ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The total amount of organic material produced by the plant, excluding water

entities.name: LEAF WATER POTENTIAL
entities.description: A measure of the energy state of water in the leaves

entities.name: STOMATAL CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The rate of passage of carbon dioxide entering, or water vapor exiting through the stomata of a leaf

entities.name: LAI
entities.description: Leaf Area Index, a dimensionless quantity that characterizes canopy structure

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATES
entities.description: The rate at which water is evaporated from the plant leaves

entities.name: NET RADIATION
entities.description: The difference between incoming and outgoing radiation measured over the canopy

entities.name: TERTIARY BRANCHES PER SECONDARY BRANCH
entities.description: The number of tertiary branches emerging from each secondary branch

entities.name: SEEDS PER PANICLE
entities.description: The number of seeds produced per panicle

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions or mass of the seeds

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The artificial application of water to the soil to assist in plant growth

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental conditions occurring after the rainy season

entities.name: FIELD STUDIES
entities.description: Research conducted in a field environment to assess plant growth and water relations

relationships.description: Irrigated sorghum exhibited higher stomatal conductance
Irrigated sorghum exhibited higher leaf water potentials
Irrigated sorghum exhibited greater DM accumulation
Increased net radiation explained higher transpiration rates in irrigated sorghum
Irrigated sorghum showed higher transpiration rates
Non-irrigated sorghum had fewer tertiary branches per secondary branch
Non-irrigated sorghum had fewer seeds per panicle
Water stress affects the growth of sorghum
Water stress affects the DM accumulation of sorghum
Sorghum was grown on deep vertisols
Sorghum was studied under post-rainy season conditions
Field studies were conducted on sorghum
